name: Pisnyi Borsch (Meatless Beet Soup)
recipeIngredient: Liquid From The Cooked Mushrooms, Optional
1 tsp Sour Salt; Crystalized Citric Acid If Not Using Kvas
1 tsp Ground Pepper; Or To Taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak dried boletus mushrooms overnight.
2. Cook in a little water until tender, then cool and chop finely.
3. Scrub beets clean and cut into quarters.
4. Cover with water and cook over low heat until tender (about 1 to 2 hours).
5. Cool and pour off the liquid, reserving it. Slip off the peels (wear rubber gloves to prevent purple hands). This can be done a day in advance.
6. Peel and cut up the other vegetables.
7. Add the bay leaf, peppercorns, and boletus or mushrooms to the vegetables with enough water to cover.
8. Cook in a large aluminum pot over low heat until tender.
9. Strain the beet liquid into the vegetables.
10. Shred the beets in a processor or on a medium grater and add.
11. Simmer for about 10 minutes, then strain into a large pot.
12. Do not press the vegetables to keep the broth clear.
13. Add the beet kvas, mushroom liquid, pepper, and salt. Bring to a gentle boil, then turn off the heat.
14. Taste; adjust seasoning if needed (tartness). For more tartness, add fresh lemon juice or sour salt.
15. Keep well in the refrigerator. Reheat gently; do not overcook or the color will turn brown.
16. To serve, pour over 3 or 4 vushka (dumplings) in soup plates and garnish with fresh dill.
